title: koeleria gracilis subvar. pseudoglauca
intro: Koeleria gracilis subvar. pseudoglauca, also known as a type of grass, is a plant species that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is primarily found in mountainous regions and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in Asian folk medicine. The plant is known to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and protecting against oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, Koeleria gracilis subvar. pseudoglauca can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety in modern medicine.
